title: Example Notebook (replace with your project)
text:
Replace this notebook with one of your real .ipynb files from the MSc course. Quarto renders .ipynb files directly — no conversion needed. Just:

Copy your real notebook into this notebooks/ folder.
Add a raw cell at the very top (like this one) with a YAML block: title, description, date, categories. This is what powers the cards on the Projects listing page.
Delete this example file once you’ve added your own.



Code
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

df = pd.DataFrame({'x': range(10), 'y': [i**2 for i in range(10)]})
df.plot(x='x', y='y', kind='line', title='Sample output (delete me)')
plt.show()

title: Example Notebook (replace with your project)
text:
Notes on execution
By default the GitHub Actions workflow re-executes every notebook from scratch when the site builds. If any of your notebooks:

read local data files — make sure those data files are also committed to the repo (e.g. in a data/ folder) and the notebook uses a relative path.
take a long time to run, or need packages beyond the common ones listed in the workflow — add the extra pip install packages to .github/workflows/publish.yml.
already have their outputs saved and you don’t want them re-run — add execute: enabled: false to this notebook’s YAML front matter so Quarto uses the notebook’s existing outputs as-is.

title: Example R Analysis (replace with your project)
text:
This is how a plain .R script becomes a page on the site. Quarto renders .qmd files (R Markdown / Quarto Markdown) using R code chunks — each chunk runs and its output (tables, plots, printed results) is embedded in the page.
Two ways to bring in your real R work:

Fastest: run scripts/convert_r_to_qmd.py path/to/your-script.R (included in this repo) — it wraps your .R file into a .qmd like this one automatically, with a title/description/date placeholder for you to fill in.
Manual: copy your R code into the chunk(s) below by hand and add the YAML header shown above (title, description, date, categories) — that header is what powers the Projects listing cards.

Two ways to bring in your real R work:

Manual: copy your R code into the chunk(s) below by hand and add the YAML header shown above (title, description, date, categories) — that header is what powers the Projects listing cards.

title: Example R Analysis (replace with your project)
text:
Data


Code
library(tidyverse)

df <- tibble(
  x = 1:10,
  y = (1:10)^2
)

title: Example R Analysis (replace with your project)
text:
Analysis


Code
ggplot(df, aes(x, y)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Replace this with your real analysis") +
  theme_minimal()

Notes

If your real script reads a data file, commit that file to the repo (e.g. a data/ folder) and reference it with a relative path.
If a script needs an R package beyond the tidyverse, add it to the packages: list in .github/workflows/publish.yml under “Install R dependencies”.
